about.title: About MagellAIn
about.subtitle: Sailing Intelligence for Great Lakes Racers
about.description: MagellAIn is a sailing intelligence platform that combines real-time weather and water data, AI-powered coaching, race management and analytics, GPS track recording and replay, race course tactical analysis, start line bias calculation, wind shift tracking, layline overlay, pre-race checklist, AI-powered route planning with custom course creation and CSV import, user feedback, admin telemetry, offline support, and safety features into a single app designed for racing sailors on Lake Erie and the Detroit River.
about.builtFor: Ford Yacht Club, West Shore Sail Club, and the DRYA racing community

features.title: Live Marine Data
features.summary: Real-time NOAA weather, NDBC buoy observations, and marine forecasts displayed on interactive charts.
features.details: Wind speed and direction, wave height, water temperature, and marine forecasts updated continuously from official government sources. Year-round shore stations include Toledo Harbor (THRO1), South Bass Island OH (SBIO1), and Marblehead OH (MRHO1). Seasonal offshore buoys — Toledo Channel Light (45132), W Lake Erie Buoy (45005), and Cleveland Buoy (45142) — deploy in late spring. Active NOAA weather alerts (Small Craft Advisories, Gale Warnings) appear as prominent banners on the Home and Weather screens.

features.title: AI Sailing Coach
features.summary: A Claude-powered assistant that streams real-time responses with live weather context, voice input, and full knowledge of your boat and local waters.
features.details: Ask about tactics, sail trim, weather interpretation, racing rules, or post-race analysis. The coach receives live NDBC buoy readings injected into its context at the time of every message — so when you ask what the wind is doing, it references actual observed conditions, not a general forecast. Responses stream token-by-token so you start reading within seconds. Tap the microphone button to ask hands-free using voice input.

features.title: AI Route Planner
features.summary: AI-powered passage planning with custom course creation, CSV waypoint import, and weather routing optimization.
features.details: Browse pre-loaded Great Lakes passages (Lake St. Clair Circuit, Ford YC to Lake Erie, Detroit River to Put-in-Bay, Port Huron to Mackinac) or create custom routes with three input modes: map click, manual coordinates, or CSV file import. Custom routes support named waypoints, notes, categories (Race, Cruise, Delivery, Practice), and public/private sharing. Edit, duplicate, or delete saved routes at any time. The AI compute engine factors in weather forecasts, your boat’s polar performance data, and PHRF rating to calculate optimal speed, sail selection, and estimated duration per leg.

features.title: Pre-Race Start Timer
features.summary: The classic 5-minute US Sailing gun sequence with audio signals, built for one-handed cockpit use.
features.details: Open the Timer tab in the bottom tab bar on the map and tap ‘5 MIN’ to start the Warning → Prep → One Minute → Start sequence. Audio beeps sound at each transition with a long horn tone at the gun. Tap ‘SYNC’ to lock the timer to an actual race committee gun. Large countdown display with color-coded phase labels — yellow for Warning, orange for Prep, red for One Minute, green for Racing.

features.title: Wind Arrow Overlay
features.summary: Live observed wind vectors drawn at every active NDBC station across Western Lake Erie and the Detroit River.
features.details: Toggle on from the map controls panel. Directional arrows appear at each monitoring station showing where the wind is blowing toward. Color scales from green (light air) through yellow, orange, and red as wind speed increases. Size also scales with speed. Tap any arrow for a station popup showing speed in knots, compass direction, degrees, and gusts.

features.title: Race Courses and Tactical Analysis
features.summary: Select any FYC or WSSC course and get leg-by-leg tactical advice using live wind data.
features.details: The Race Courses panel on the Map screen lets you choose from all active race courses stored in the database. Selecting a course shows its legs: mark name, rounding direction, bearing, and distance. Toggle course marks on the chart as colored pins. Tap ‘AI Tactical Analysis’ to see each leg’s point of sail calculated from current NDBC wind direction, with specific tactical advice for conditions.

features.title: Start Line Bias Tool
features.summary: Mark the two ends of your start line on the chart and instantly see which end is favored based on live wind data.
features.details: Open the Start Line Bias tool from the Tools tab in the bottom drawer on the map. Tap ‘Boat / Pin End’ then tap its position on the chart, then do the same for ‘Committee.’ MagellAIn calculates the bias angle and tells you which end is favored and by how many degrees. The line draws on the chart in amber with color-coded pins. Wind direction is pulled automatically from the nearest active NDBC buoy.

features.title: Wind Shift Tracking
features.summary: See the last 90 minutes of wind direction history from any active NDBC station as a live chart on the map or weather screen.
features.details: The Wind Shift panel on the Map screen and the Wind Shift History section on the Weather page show a time-series chart of wind direction over the last 90 minutes from any NDBC shore station you select. The chart highlights veering or backing trends and shows the current wind speed.

features.title: Layline Overlay
features.summary: See port and starboard laylines to the windward mark drawn directly on the chart, with an adjustable tacking angle.
features.details: Enable laylines from the Layers panel in map controls. Green dashes show port tack, red dashes show starboard tack. The tacking angle slider (30-55 degrees, default 42) lets you tune for your boat class and conditions. Laylines update automatically as wind direction changes from NDBC observations.

features.title: Pre-Race Checklist
features.summary: A comprehensive 30-item race preparation checklist covering rig, sails, safety, boat systems, and race day specifics.
features.details: Five accordion sections — Rig, Sails, Safety, Boat, and Race Day — contain the key items every racing sailor should verify before the start gun. A live progress bar tracks completion. Session-based and designed for one-handed use on the water.

features.title: GPS Track Recording
features.summary: Record your track on the water with live speed, distance, and time stats.
features.details: The GPS Tracker panel on the Map screen lets you start recording your position, speed (in knots), and heading using your device GPS. Live stats show elapsed time, current speed, total nautical miles, and max speed. Tracks save to your account when signed in, or to local device storage for offline use.

features.title: Track Replay
features.summary: Replay any saved GPS track on the map with animated playback and variable speed.
features.details: Select any saved track to draw the full route in orange on the chart. Play, Pause, or Stop the animated dot as it moves through your recorded positions. Choose 1x, 5x, 20x, or 50x playback speed.

features.title: Race Management and Analytics
features.summary: Club schedules, results, series standings, CSV result import, head-to-head records, and performance trend charts.
features.details: View upcoming and past races from Ford YC, West Shore SC, and DRYA series. Import race results from any CSV export using the four-step wizard. The Performance screen shows 19 metrics per boat including season trend chart, series standings, and head-to-head win/loss records with PHRF correction details. Export your race log as a PDF.

features.title: Custom Route Creation
features.summary: Build your own sailing routes with drag-and-drop map editing, manual coordinate entry, or CSV file import.
features.details: The route creator supports three input modes. Map mode: tap the chart to place waypoints, drag markers to adjust. Coords mode: type exact lat/lng for precision. CSV mode: upload a file with waypoint coordinates — MagellAIn auto-detects delimiters and column headers. Routes are categorized (Race, Cruise, Delivery, Practice), can be public or private, and support named waypoints with notes. Edit, duplicate, or delete routes from the My Routes panel.

features.title: User Feedback
features.summary: Send suggestions, bug reports, or help requests directly to the MagellAIn team from within the app.
features.details: Go to Menu > Send Feedback to submit feedback in four categories: Suggestion, Bug Report, Need Help, or Other. Add a subject, detailed message (up to 5,000 characters), and optional email for follow-up. No sign-in required. All feedback is tracked and reviewed by the admin team through the telemetry dashboard.

features.title: Offline Support
features.summary: The app stays functional when you lose signal — cached pages, offline GPS tracking, and an offline indicator.
features.details: A service worker caches all main screens and recently loaded data so you can keep using MagellAIn underway without a connection. GPS track recording works fully offline. An amber banner appears when offline and clears automatically when you reconnect. NOAA data remains available from cache for up to one hour.

features.title: Safety First
features.summary: Emergency USCG contact, SOS text with GPS coordinates, float plans, and weather alert banners.
features.details: One-tap calling to 911, USCG Sector Detroit (313-568-9564), and USCG Station Toledo (419-691-4048). Get your GPS coordinates displayed for relay to rescue services. Send an SOS text with your Google Maps location link. File digital float plans pre-filled with your vessel details.

features.title: Live Sailing News
features.summary: A curated sailing news feed on the home screen pulling from top publications.
features.details: Recent articles from Sail Magazine, Scuttlebutt Sailing News, World Sailing, and Sailing World. Articles show title, excerpt, source, image, and time since publication.

features.title: Built for the Water
features.summary: Clean, one-handed mobile interface that works when conditions are tough.
features.details: Designed for use on a wet, moving boat with one hand. Large touch targets, high-contrast dark and light display themes, and voice input when your hands are full. No clutter, no distractions — a professional tool for serious sailors.

dataSources.name: NOAA / NWS
dataSources.description: Marine weather forecasts, zone alerts (Small Craft Advisory, Gale Warning), and National Weather Service gridpoint data for Lake Erie

dataSources.name: NDBC Buoys
dataSources.description: Real-time wind, wave, water temperature, and pressure observations from active Great Lakes monitoring stations

dataSources.name: NOAA CO-OPS
dataSources.description: Water levels and tidal current data for the Detroit River

dataSources.name: YachtScoring / PHRF-LE
dataSources.description: Race results and regatta data importable via CSV export from local clubs

dataSources.name: Supabase
dataSources.description: Cloud database for race courses, marks, regatta results, GPS tracks, boat profiles, routes, telemetry, and user data

legal.disclaimer: MagellAIn is a decision-support tool, not a substitute for seamanship, proper safety equipment, or sound judgment. Weather data is sourced from NOAA and may be delayed. Always monitor VHF Channel 16 and carry required safety gear. The emergency feature connects to USCG but does not replace a VHF radio or EPIRB.
legal.dataAttribution: Weather and marine data provided by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), National Weather Service (NWS), and National Data Buoy Center (NDBC).
